recaps.text: Previously, in The Blade Itself and Before They Are Hanged: Bayaz, First of the Magi, gathered an unlikely company - the Northman Logen Ninefingers, the crippled Inquisitor Glokta, the once-vain officer Jezal dan Luthar, and the vengeful Ferro Maljinn - and led some of them on a long, brutal journey to the edge of the World in search of an ancient weapon, the Seed, meant to turn back the Gurkish Empire and its prophet Khalul. The quest failed, and the true nature and ruthlessness of the wizard grew ever harder to ignore. Meanwhile Glokta fought and lost to hold the doomed southern city of Dagoska; in the North, Crown Prince Ladisla's army was destroyed and Major West, thrown among Logen's old crew, was driven to a desperate act to survive. Now, with the Gurkish massing and the North still at war, the scattered survivors return to a Union sliding toward crisis.

The company is home, and the Union is coming apart at the seams. In the North, Logen rejoins the Dogman and the old crew to press the war against Bethod at last toward its reckoning. In Adua, the old King is failing and the succession is unresolved, and Glokta is set to dangerous work amid the scheming of the Arch Lector Sult and the hidden financiers who hold the realm's debts. Jezal, changed by his travels, wants only a quiet life he will not be allowed; Ferro's private vengeance draws nearer the open war with the Gurkish; and Bayaz begins, quietly, to move his own pieces into place.

The crises break into the open. The Gurkish invasion gathers to a head, and Adua itself comes under threat as the enemy closes on the capital. The question of who will wear the Union's crown becomes a battlefield of its own, with Glokta manoeuvring among factions far more powerful and dangerous than he, at constant risk of his life. In the North, the war against Bethod drives toward its bloody climax, and the Bloody-Nine is never far beneath Logen's skin. Bayaz's long-laid schemes begin to show their shape, and it grows clearer what the old wizard is willing to sacrifice to win.

Everything comes due. The struggle for the Union's throne is settled in a way few could have foreseen, and the cost of Bayaz's victory over the Gurkish - and of the ancient power he is finally willing to unleash - falls hard on Adua and on the people who trusted him. In the North, the war against Bethod reaches its end, and Logen's reckoning with what he is leaves him, once more, with little of what he wanted. Old debts are paid in blood and betrayal; some of the company find a kind of peace, others ruin or death, and the wizard's grand design stands revealed for what it always was. The world is left much as the powerful intended it - and the reader is left to judge who, if anyone, truly won.
